title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 0)
text:
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 1 of 11 
 
STATE OF CALIFORNIA ) 
ORDINANCE NO. 25-08 
COUNTY OF RIVERSIDE ) ss 
CITY OF SAN JACINTO ) 
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY OF SAN JACINTO, CALIFORNIA, 
AMENDING 
AND 
ADDING 
CERTAIN 
SECTIONS 
AND 
SUBSECTIONS IN TITLE 15 (BUILDINGS AND CONSTRUCTION) OF 
THE SAN JACINTO MUNICIPAL CODE PERTAINING TO THE 
CONSTRUCTION, 
ALTERATION, 
AND 
MAINTENANCE 
OF 
BUILDINGS AND STRUCTURES, AND ADOPTING BY REFERENCE 
THE 2025 EDITION OF THE CALIFORNIA BUILDING STANDARDS 
CODE, KNOWN AS TITLE 24 OF THE CALIFORNIA CODE OF 
REGULATIONS, INCLUDING THE 2025 CALIFORNIA BUILDING 
CODE 
(INCORPORATING 
AND 
AMENDING 
THE 
2024 
INTERNATIONAL BUILDING CODE), THE 2025 CALIFORNIA 
ELECTRICAL CODE (INCORPORATING AND AMENDING THE 2023 
NATIONAL 
ELECTRICAL 
CODE), 
THE 
2025 
CALIFORNIA 
MECHANICAL CODE (INCORPORATING AND AMENDING THE 2024 
UNIFORM 
MECHANICAL 
CODE), 
THE 
2025 
CALIFORNIA 
PLUMBING CODE (INCORPORATING AND AMENDING THE 2024 
UNIFORM 
PLUMBING 
CODE), 
THE 
2025 
CALIFORNIA 
ADMINISTRATIVE CODE, THE 2025 CALIFORNIA ENERGY CODE, 
THE 2025 CALIFORNIA GREEN BUILDING STANDARDS CODE, THE 
2025 REFERENCED STANDARDS CODE, AND THE 2024 UNIFORM 
SWIMMING POOL, SPA AND HOT TUB CODE, TOGETHER WITH 
LOCAL AMENDMENTS, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS BASED ON 
LOCAL 
CLIMATIC, 
GEOLOGICAL, 
AND 
TOPOGRAPHICAL 
CONDITIONS, 
AND 
REPEALING 
OBSOLETE 
PROVISIONS 
RELATING TO THE 1997 UNIFORM ADMINISTRATIVE CODE. 
WHEREAS, pursuant to California Government Code Sections 50022.1 et seq., 
the City of San Jacinto (“City”) may adopt by reference the California Building Standards 
Code, as set forth in Title 24 of the California Code of Regulations; and 
WHEREAS, the California Building Standards Commission (“CBSC”) has 
published the 2025 Edition of the California Building Standards Code, which becomes 
effective statewide on January 1, 2026; and 
WHEREAS, California Health and Safety Code Sections 17958, 17958.5, 17958.7, 
and 18941.5 authorize cities and counties to

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 1)
text:
g Standards Code, which becomes 
effective statewide on January 1, 2026; and 
WHEREAS, California Health and Safety Code Sections 17958, 17958.5, 17958.7, 
and 18941.5 authorize cities and counties to make modifications to the California Building 
Standards Code where reasonably necessary because of local climatic, geological, or 
topographical conditions; and 
WHEREAS, the City Council finds that the proposed amendments to the 2025 
California Building Standards Code set forth in this Ordinance are more restrictive than 
the standards adopted by the California Building Standards Commission, would reduce 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 2 of 11 
 
the potential for property damage, injury, and loss of life, and are reasonable and 
necessary to mitigate local conditions; and 
WHEREAS, the City Council introduced this Ordinance No. 25-08 on October 21, 
2025, at which time all interested persons were given the opportunity to appear and be 
heard; and 
WHEREAS, the City Council held a duly noticed public hearing on November 18, 
2025 to consider the adoption of the 2025 California Building Standards Code and local 
amendments; and 
WHEREAS, copies of each uniform code to be adopted by reference have been 
on file with the City Clerk of the City of San Jacinto for at least fifteen (15) days prior to 
the public hearing and are available for public inspection, as required by Government 
Code Section 50022.6; and 
WHEREAS, all legal prerequisites relating to the adoption of this Ordinance have 
occurred. 
THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF SAN JACINTO DOES HEREBY ORDAIN AS 
FOLLOWS: 
Section 1. Findings. The following changes and modifications to the 2025 California 
Building Standards Code are more restrictive than the standards contained 
in the 2025 California Building Standards Code and therefore require 
findings based on local conditions. The City Council hereby finds and 
determines that, due to local climatic, ge

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 2)
text:
dards contained 
in the 2025 California Building Standards Code and therefore require 
findings based on local conditions. The City Council hereby finds and 
determines that, due to local climatic, geological, and topographical 
conditions, the following changes and modifications are reasonably 
necessary to provide sufficient and effective protection of life, health, and 
property. The City Council adopts the findings set forth below to support the 
modifications to the 2025 California Building Standards Code. 
A. Amendments to Chapter 1, Section 105.2, “Building” Item 2 of the 
2025 Edition of the California Building Code are reasonably 
necessary due to the following local climatic condition: The City of 
San Jacinto is subject to frequent high-velocity Santa Ana winds 
exceeding 100 miles per hour, which have historically caused 
property damage to fences and walls. The local amendment clarifies 
which fences are exempt from permitting and ensures that retaining 
walls or structural fence systems exposed to these winds are 
properly reviewed for safety and anchorage. 
B. Amendments to Chapter 1, Section 105.5, “Expiration of Permits,” 
are reasonably necessary due to the following local administrative 
conditions: The City regularly encounters long-term project inactivity 
resulting from construction delays, changes in ownership, and 
unpermitted work stoppages. Establishing clear local limits on permit 
duration and extensions ensures that inactive or abandoned permits 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 3 of 11 
 
do not remain open indefinitely, allowing the City to maintain 
accurate records and effective code enforcement. 
C. Amendments to Chapter 16 of the California Building Code regarding 
local wind design standards are reasonably necessary due to the 
following local climatic and topographical conditions: The San 
Jacinto Valley is characterized by open terrain, mountain passes, 
and strong prevailing

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 3)
text:
standards are reasonably necessary due to the 
following local climatic and topographical conditions: The San 
Jacinto Valley is characterized by open terrain, mountain passes, 
and strong prevailing winds. To mitigate the potential for structural 
damage to masonry walls and other non-engineered elements, the 
City retains a basic wind design speed of one hundred (100) miles 
per hour, consistent with regional historical data and previous 
adopted standards. 
D. Amendments to Chapter 1, Section 109.1, “Plan Review Fees,” are 
reasonably necessary due to the following local administrative 
condition: The City maintains a Master Fee Schedule adopted by 
resolution to allow fees to be periodically updated in accordance with 
cost-recovery policies and inflation adjustments. Referencing the 
Master Fee Schedule within the Building Code provides flexibility and 
ensures fees remain aligned with actual service costs without 
requiring separate ordinance amendments. 
E. All other amendments to Title 15 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code 
adopted by this Ordinance are hereby found, declared, and 
determined by the City Council to be primarily administrative, 
procedural, or clarifying in nature, and therefore equivalent in 
purpose and intent to the corresponding provisions of the 2025 
California Building Standards Code. 
Section 2. Section 15.04.010 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby repealed in 
its entirety. 
Section 3. Section 15.04.020 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby amended 
in its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.020 2025 Building Code adopted—Modifications. 
The California Building Code, 2025 Edition, including Chapter 1 and all 
appendices, tables, and indices thereto (copies of which are on file in the 
Office of the City Clerk), is hereby adopted as part of the Building Code of 
the City of San Jacinto with the following modifications: 
A. Section 105.2, Building, Item 1 is amended to read as follows: 
“1. One-story detached accessory s

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 4)
text:
dopted as part of the Building Code of 
the City of San Jacinto with the following modifications: 
A. Section 105.2, Building, Item 1 is amended to read as follows: 
“1. One-story detached accessory structures used as tool and 
storage sheds, playhouses and similar uses, provided the floor area 
does not exceed 120 square feet (11.15 m2). It is permissible that 
these structures still be regulated by Section 710A, despite 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 4 of 11 
 
exemption from permit. However, a permit is required for any 
concrete flat work associated with the structures.” 
B. Section 105.2, Building, Item 2 is amended to read as follows: 
“2. Fences and walls accessory to Group R-3 occupancies and 
located on lots used exclusively for single-family residential purposes 
shall not require a building permit where the height does not exceed 
six (6) feet measured from adjacent grade, provided they comply with 
the San Jacinto Municipal Code, Development Code (Title 17), and 
California Building Standards Code. Retaining walls over eighteen 
(18) inches in height, and any fence or wall that includes a retaining 
wall over eighteen (18) inches in height with a freestanding fence on 
top, shall require a building permit.” 
C. Section 105.2, Building, Item 5 is amended to read as follows: 
“5. Water tanks supported directly on grade if the capacity does not 
exceed 5,000 gallons (18,925 L) and the ratio of height to diameter 
or width does not exceed 2:1 and any associated concrete flat work.” 
D. Section 105.5 is amended in its entirety to read as follows: 
“Permits shall expire where the work authorized has not commenced 
within one hundred eighty (180) days of issuance, or where work is 
suspended or abandoned for one hundred eighty (180) days. The 
Building Official may grant a single extension for good cause not to 
exceed one hundred eighty (180) days. Additional extensions require 
written request and appro

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 5)
text:
one hundred eighty (180) days. The 
Building Official may grant a single extension for good cause not to 
exceed one hundred eighty (180) days. Additional extensions require 
written request and approval by the Building Official.” 
E. Section 109.1 (Payment of Fees) is amended to read as follows: 
“A permit shall not be valid until the fees prescribed by law have been 
paid, nor shall an amendment to a permit be released until the 
additional fee, if any, has been paid.  When plans or other data are 
required to be submitted under Section 107, a plan review fee shall 
be paid at the time of submittal. Plan review fees shall be calculated 
and collected in accordance with the City’s Master Fee Schedule, as 
adopted by City Council resolution and as may be amended from 
time to time. The fee shall cover the cost of examining plans, 
specifications, and supporting documents for compliance with 
applicable codes and standards. 
Where plans are incomplete, substantially revised, or resubmitted for 
additional review, an additional plan review fee may be assessed in 
accordance with the City’s Master Fee Schedule.” 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 5 of 11 
 
F. F. 
Section 1616.3.3 is added to Chapter 16, “Structural Design,” 
of the CBC is amended to include the following local provisionread 
as follows: 
“1616.3.3 Block Walls. Block walls within the  City shall be designed 
for a basic wind speed of one hundred (100) miles per hour, due to 
documented local wind conditions and historic regional wind events.” 
G. Appendix J (Grading), Section J110.3 is hereby added to read as 
follows: 
“J110.3 Slopes.  The faces of cut and fill slopes shall be prepared 
and maintained to control erosion and provide stability. Where cut 
slopes are not subject to erosion due to erosion-resistant character 
of materials, such production may be omitted. Unless otherwise 
recommended in the approved soil engineering or engineering 
geol

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 6)
text:
slopes are not subject to erosion due to erosion-resistant character 
of materials, such production may be omitted. Unless otherwise 
recommended in the approved soil engineering or engineering 
geology report, cut and fill slopes shall be planted in accordance with 
this Section. The protection for the slopes shall be installed as soon 
as practicable and prior to calling for final permit approval. 
A. 
General Requirements.  Cut slopes equal to or greater than 5 
feet in vertical height and fill slopes equal to or greater than 3 
feet in vertical height shall be planted with grass or ground 
cover to protect the slope from erosion and instability. Other 
slopes as deemed necessary by the Community Development 
Director shall also be planted. 
B. 
Slopes exceeding 15 feet in vertical height shall be planted 
with shrubs, spaced at not more than 10 feet on center; or 
trees, spaced not to exceed 20 feet on center; or a combination 
of shrubs and trees at equivalent spacings, in addition to the 
grass or ground cover. The plants selected and planting 
patterns may be varied upon the recommendation of a 
landscape architect or a slope control specialist with approval 
of the Community Development Director. If a species other 
than those from the recommended list of plants is selected, a 
written statement shall be submitted by a landscape architect 
or slope control specialist certifying the plants suitable for 
erosion control and slope stability. This statement must 
accompany the grading plan at the time of submittal. 
C. 
All Landscaping shall be in accordance with Chapter 17.325 of 
the Development Code.  
Section 4. Section 15.04.030 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby amended in 
its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.030 2025 Mechanical Code adopted—Modifications. 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 6 of 11 
 
The California Mechanical Code, 2025 Edition, including Chapter 1 and all 
appendices, ta

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 7)
text:
ifications. 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 6 of 11 
 
The California Mechanical Code, 2025 Edition, including Chapter 1 and all 
appendices, tables, and indices thereto (copies of which are on file in the 
Office of the City Clerk), is adopted as the Mechanical Code of the City of 
San Jacinto.” 
Section 5. Section 15.04.035 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby amended in 
its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.035 2025 Residential Code adopted—Modifications. 
The California Residential Code, 2025 Edition, including Chapter 1 and all 
appendices, tables, and indices thereto (copies of which are on file in the 
Office of the City Clerk), is adopted as the Residential Code of the City of 
San Jacinto.” 
Section 6. Section 15.04.040 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby amended in 
its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.040 2025 Plumbing Code adopted—Modifications. 
The California Plumbing Code, 2025 Edition, including Chapter 1 and all 
appendices, tables, and indices thereto (copies of which are on file in the 
Office of the City Clerk), is adopted as the Plumbing Code of the City of San 
Jacinto.” 
Section 7. Section 15.04.045 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby amended in 
its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.045 2025 Green Building Standards Code adopted—Modifications. 
The California Green Building Standards Code, 2025 Edition, and including 
all appendices, tables, and indices, but excepting any voluntary provisions 
thereto (copies of which are on file in the Office of the City Clerk), is adopted 
as the Green Building Standards Code of the City of San Jacinto.” 
Section 8. Section 15.04.055 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby amended in 
its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.055 2025 Administrative Code adopted—Modifications. 
The California Administrative Code, 2025 Edition, and including all 
appendices, tables, and indices, but excepting any voluntary provisions

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 8)
text:
“15.04.055 2025 Administrative Code adopted—Modifications. 
The California Administrative Code, 2025 Edition, and including all 
appendices, tables, and indices, but excepting any voluntary provisions 
thereto (copies of which are on file in the Office of the City Clerk), is adopted 
as the Administrative Code of the City of San Jacinto.” 
Section 9. Section 15.04.065 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby amended in 
its entirety to read as follows: 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 7 of 11 
 
“15.04.065 2025 Energy Code adopted—Modifications. 
The California Energy Code, 2025 Edition, and including all appendices, 
tables, and indices, but excepting any voluntary provisions thereto (copies 
of which are on file in the Office of the City Clerk), is adopted as the Energy 
Code of the City of San Jacinto.” 
Section 10. 
Section 15.04.070 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby 
amended in its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.070 Uniform Swimming Pool, Spa and Hot Tub Code adopted. 
The Uniform Swimming Pool, Spa and Hot Tub Code, 2024 Edition, and all 
appendices, tables, and indices thereto (copies of which are on file in the 
Office of the City Clerk), is adopted as the Uniform Swimming Pool, Spa 
and Hot Tub Code of the City of San Jacinto.” 
Section 11. 
Section 15.04.075 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby 
amended in its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.075 2025 Referenced Standards Code adopted—Modifications. 
The California Referenced Standards Code, 2025 Edition, and including all 
appendices, tables, and indices, but excepting any voluntary provisions 
thereto (copies of which are on file in the Office of the City Clerk), is adopted 
as the Referenced Standards Code of the City of San Jacinto.” 
Section 12. 
Section 15.04.080 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby 
amended in its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.080 2025 Electrical Code adopted—Modifications. 
The Califo

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 9)
text:
n Jacinto.” 
Section 12. 
Section 15.04.080 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby 
amended in its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.080 2025 Electrical Code adopted—Modifications. 
The California Electrical Code, 2025 Edition, including Article 89 and all 
appendices, tables, and indices thereto (copies of which are on file in the 
Office of the City Clerk), is adopted as the Electrical Code of the City of San 
Jacinto.” 
Section 13. 
Section 15.04.115 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby 
amended in its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.115 Enforcement—Stop-work orders. 
The Building Official is authorized to issue stop-work orders where 
construction is occurring without a permit or in violation of the conditions of 
a permit. Work shall not resume until the violation has been corrected, 
applicable fees have been paid, and the stop-work order has been 
rescinded by the Building Official.” 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 8 of 11 
 
Section 14. 
Section 15.04.120 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby 
amended in its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.04.120 Violation—Penalty. 
A. Penalties. It shall be unlawful for any person, firm, or corporation to erect, 
construct, enlarge, alter, repair, move, improve, remove, convert, demolish, 
equip, use, occupy, or maintain any building or structure, or cause or permit 
the same to be done, in violation of this Code. 
B. Violation of the provisions of this chapter or the 2025 Building Code is 
deemed to be a misdemeanor punishable by fine or imprisonment, or by 
both fine and imprisonment. A person is guilty of a separate offense each 
day during which he or she commits, continues, or permits a violation of this 
chapter or the 2025 Building Code. 
C. Violations of this chapter are declared to be a public nuisance, and the 
City Attorney may institute necessary legal procedures for the abatement, 
removal, or enjoinment thereof in the manner pr

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 10)
text:
C. Violations of this chapter are declared to be a public nuisance, and the 
City Attorney may institute necessary legal procedures for the abatement, 
removal, or enjoinment thereof in the manner provided by law, or may take 
such other steps as deemed necessary to accomplish these ends. The 
application of any penalty shall not be held to prevent the enforced removal 
of prohibited conditions.” 
Section 15. 
Section 15.08.010 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby 
amended in its entirety to read as follows: 
“15.08.010 Application requirements—Electronic submittals. 
Applications for building permits, including plans and supporting 
documents, shall be submitted electronically in a format approved by the 
Building Official. Electronic submittals shall have the same force and effect 
as paper plans and shall be retained in accordance with the City’s records 
retention policy.” 
Section 16. 
Section 15.08.020 of the San Jacinto Municipal Code is hereby 
amended in its entirety to read as follows  
“15.08.020 – Fees and Service Charges. 
All fees and service charges for permits, plan review, inspections, re-
inspections, energy compliance, and other services required by this Title or 
by the California Building Standards Code shall be established by resolution 
of the City Council. Such fees shall be based on the reasonable cost of 
providing the service, as determined by the City, and may be adjusted from 
time to time by Council resolution.” 
Section 17. 
Sections 15.08.025 is hereby added to the San Jacinto Municipal 
Code to read as follows. 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 9 of 11 
 
“15.08.025 Fees in Effect Unless Superseded. 
The various fees established in this chapter shall remain in effect and 
enforceable unless and until amended, superseded, or repealed by 
resolution of the City Council. 
Where the City Council has adopted a fee by resolution that differs from the 
amount specified in this ch

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 11)
text:
rceable unless and until amended, superseded, or repealed by 
resolution of the City Council. 
Where the City Council has adopted a fee by resolution that differs from the 
amount specified in this chapter, the fee established by resolution shall 
govern.” 
Section 18. 
Section 15.08.130 is hereby added to the San Jacinto Municipal 
Code to read as follows: 
“15.08.1530 Contractor license and City business license requirement; 
owner-builder limitation. 
A. All building permits for construction, alteration, or repair of buildings or 
structures, other than single-family dwellings and their accessory 
structures, shall be issued only to a contractor licensed pursuant to the 
California Contractors State License Law (Business and Professions Code 
§ 7000 et seq.). 
B. The licensed contractor shall maintain a valid City of San Jacinto 
business license at the time of permit issuance and throughout the duration 
of work authorized by the permit. 
C. Owner-builder permits shall be limited to single-family dwellings (Group 
R-3 occupancy) and their accessory structures, as defined in the California 
Residential Code.” 
Section 19. 
CEQA. The City Council finds that this Ordinance is not subject to 
the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) pursuant to Sections 
15060(c)(2) and 15060(c)(3) of the CEQA Guidelines (California Code of 
Regulations, Title 14, Chapter 3), because adoption of the 2025 California 
Building Standards Code and related local amendments will not result in a 
direct or reasonably foreseeable indirect physical change in the 
environment. This action is a regulatory update mandated by State law and 
therefore does not constitute a “project” as defined in Section 15378 of the 
CEQA Guidelines. 
Section 20. 
Severability. Should any provision, section, paragraph, sentence, or 
word of this chapter be determined or declared invalid by any final court 
action in a court of competent jurisdiction or by reason of any preemptive 
legislation, the remaini

title: san_jacinto_2025 (window 12)
text:
paragraph, sentence, or 
word of this chapter be determined or declared invalid by any final court 
action in a court of competent jurisdiction or by reason of any preemptive 
legislation, the remaining provisions, sections, paragraphs, sentences, or 
words of this chapter shall remain in full force and effect. 
Section 21. 
Publication.  The City Clerk shall certify as to the adoption of this 
Ordinance and shall cause a summary thereof to be published within fifteen 
Docusign Envelope ID: 7EB826AD-A32F-403A-85DC-D1D3F53434D5
Ordinance No. 25-08 
 
Page 10 of 11 
 
(15) days of the adoption and shall post a Certified copy of this Ordinance, 
including the vote for and against the same, in the Office of the City Clerk, 
in accordance with Government Code Section 36933. 
Section 22. 
Effective Date.  This Ordinance shall take effect and be in full force 
on January 1, 2026, concurrent with the effective date of the 2025 California 
Building Standards Code, as adopted by the State of California. 
Introduced at a regular meeting of the City Council on the 21st day of October 2025. 
 
PASSED APPROVED, AND ADOPTED at a regular meeting of the City Council 
on the 18th Day of November 2025, by the following vote. 
 
AYES:  
Ayala, Ledezma, Sanchez, Ruiz, Vandever
